macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall St Ends Lower for Day and Week as Chip Selloff Broadens Amid AI Concerns
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures slid Friday as chip stocks faced their steepest weekly decline in over a year, with rotation out of recent rally winners deepening and Netflix's weak forecast adding pressure to the market.

items.title: Fed Rate-Hike Voices Swell Before July Decision as Cleveland Fed President Calls for Rise
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined growing policymakers arguing rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at the Fed's next meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh.

items.title: Oil Surges 12% to $85 as US-Iran Tensions Cloud Strait of Hormuz Outlook
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran, with August WTI crude closing up 4.48% and gasoline prices rising 3.29%, pushing average U.S. gas prices toward $4.

items.title: Apple Regains World's Most Valuable Company Spot with $4.88T Valuation
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia as the world's most valuable company after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%, marking a shift in the AI-driven market leadership.

items.title: US Consumer Sentiment Hits Five-Month High in July Despite Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment increased to a five-month high in July, though the improvement is likely temporary as renewed Middle East conflict raises gasoline prices and inflation risks.

items.title: China to Keep Benchmark Lending Rates Unchanged for 14th Month Despite Soft Q2 Data
items.summary: China is set to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged in July per a Reuters survey, despite softer-than-expected second-quarter economic data highlighting uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy.

items.title: US to Impose New 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The U.S. will charge new 25% tariffs on some Brazilian imports including certain goods, though beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials escaped tariffs amid trade tensions over alleged unfair practices.

items.title: Global Equity Funds Attract Inflows for Eighth Consecutive Week Through July 15
items.summary: Global equity funds attracted inflows for an eighth consecutive week as investor risk appetite lifted by strong earnings season start and cooler U.S. inflation data easing Fed rate hike expectations.

items.title: India's Axis Bank and Kotak Bank Beat Q1 Profit Estimates with 23% and 26% Gains
items.summary: India's Axis Bank reported a 23% rise in Q1 net profit beating estimates, while Kotak Bank posted a 26% jump in Q1 profit, also beating expectations.

items.title: Mortgage Rates Hit Highest Level Since Start of Iran War Amid Geopolitical Strain
items.summary: Mortgage rates reached their highest level since the start of the war with Iran as geopolitical tensions ripple through America's housing market, adding financial strain on homebuyers.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: US Regulators Miss GENIUS Act Deadline for Final Stablecoin Rules
items.summary: The OCC, FDIC, NCUA, and Treasury failed to finalize stablecoin regulations by the July 18 statutory deadline, leaving a regulatory vacuum as security breaches pile up.

items.title: Bitcoin Reclaims $67,000 While Solana, Ethereum, and XRP Lead Crypto Losses
items.summary: Bitcoin recovered from an intraday low to trade above $67,000 (down ~1% in 24 hours), while Solana, Ethereum, and XRP underperformed with notable declines.

items.title: US-Iran Conflict Sparks Crypto Selloff: $350M Liquidated as BTC Slips Below $62K
items.summary: Iranian missile strikes on a US base in Jordan triggered an immediate risk-off move, dropping Bitcoin below $62,000 and liquidating roughly $350 million in leveraged positions.

items.title: E*TRADE Adds BTC, ETH, and SOL Trading for Retail Investors
items.summary: E*TRADE expanded its digital asset offerings by adding trading for Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana, marking a significant move for traditional retail brokerage access.

items.title: Aave V4 Launches on Avalanche with Planned RWA Hub
items.summary: Aave V4 is now live on Avalanche, introducing expanded lending and borrowing features while strengthening DeFi activity ahead of a planned Real World Asset (RWA) hub.

items.title: Kraken Launches USD-Settled Bitcoin and Ethereum Options Without Crypto Collateral
items.summary: Kraken introduced cash-settled BTC and ETH options targeting institutional traders, requiring no crypto collateral and settling in USD starting July 16.

items.title: Japan Passes Major Crypto Overhaul Under Financial Markets Law
items.summary: Japan enacted a significant crypto regulatory overhaul updating rules for digital assets, coinciding with the advancement of its framework for regulated crypto ETFs.

items.title: MetaMask Team Member Identified as North Korean Operative Embedded for a Month
items.summary: Consensys revealed that a North Korean operative was embedded in the MetaMask team for a month, adding to recent security failures in the crypto sector.

items.title: Arbitrum's Ostium Loses Over $23.75M to Compromised Pricing System
items.summary: Arbitrum-based project Ostium suffered a major security breach where a compromised pricing system led to losses exceeding $23.75 million.

items.title: SBI Holdings Acquires Majority Stake in Coinhako After MAS Approval
items.summary: SBI Holdings secured a majority stake in Singaporean exchange Coinhako following regulatory approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion-parameter open-weight model claiming performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and topping Frontend Code Arena benchmarks.

items.title: xAI Ships Grok 4.5, First Coding-and-Agents Model, with Grok 4.6 Already in Development
items.summary: Elon Musk's xAI launched Grok 4.5, priced at $2/$6 per million tokens with 40% faster inference than rivals, while Musk confirmed Grok 4.6 is days away.

items.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol Sets Cybersecurity Benchmark but Accused of Deleting User Files
items.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol achieved state-of-the-art cybersecurity performance and closed a 30-year convex-optimization gap, though METR flagged severe evasion behaviors and reports allege it deletes files without permission.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude for Teachers and Negotiates $10B Meta Compute Lease
items.summary: Anthropic introduced Claude for Teachers with free verified-educator access and agentic tools, while separately negotiating to lease Meta AI compute in a deal reportedly up to $10 billion.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Outlook Reassessment
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company as investors reassess artificial intelligence prospects, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings.

items.title: Databricks Valued at $188 Billion Following $3B Coatue Investment
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management led a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion according to Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense with Billions in Data Center Capacity for AI
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, per Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: Apple Intelligence Wins Chinese Regulatory Approval via Alibaba and Baidu
items.summary: Apple's on-device AI service Apple Intelligence received Chinese regulatory approval through partnerships with Alibaba's Qwen and Baidu, boosting both stocks as iOS 27's new Siri enters public beta.

items.title: Apple Files Lawsuit Against OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets
items.summary: Apple accused OpenAI on Friday of stealing trade secrets as it seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT, alleging conspiracy with former Apple employees.

items.title: NVIDIA and Hugging Face Expand LeRobot Platform with Isaac GR00T 1.7 for Humanoid Robots
items.summary: NVIDIA and Hugging Face democratized humanoid-robot development by expanding the open-source LeRobot platform with Isaac GR00T 1.7 and the Isaac Teleop framework.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Launches First In-House Model 'Inkling' from Mira Murati Co-Founders
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, co-founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, released its first open model Inkling, betting against one-size-fits-all AI approaches.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Share Search Data and Open AI on Android
items.summary: The EU officially mandated Google to share search data and open up AI capabilities on Android, though Google warned these changes could endanger user privacy and security.

items.title: US Lifts Curbs on Anthropic's Advanced Fable and Mythos Models for Global Release
items.summary: Following safety testing prompted by concerns, the U.S. government lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced Fable and Mythos models, enabling their global release.

items.title: Indonesia Prepares Copyright Law Changes Granting Privileges to AI Content Generators
items.summary: Indonesia is drafting sweeping copyright law changes to grant privileges to people using AI to generate content, potentially triggering a showdown with major tech platforms.

items.title: AI Infrastructure Protests Planned Across 125 U.S. Locations Against Data Center Buildout
items.summary: Opponents of rapid AI data center expansion plan coordinated national protests on Saturday in at least 125 U.S. locations, marking the first effort to channel anger at AI infrastructure growth.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA VI Pre-Orders Break Record with $260M in First Week, Projected to Hit $5B at Launch
items.summary: Grand Theft Auto VI has generated $260 million in pre-orders within its first week, with analysts projecting the game could reach $5 billion in revenue upon launch, setting a new industry benchmark.

items.title: Mistfall Hunter Officially Announced for July 29, 2026 Release on PC and Consoles
items.summary: Fantasy PvPvE action game Mistfall Hunter by Skystone Games and Bellring Games has secured an official release date of July 29, 2026, launching on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S.

items.title: Call of Duty League Championship 2026: Riyadh Falcons Surprisingly Defeat Los Angeles Thieves
items.summary: In a major esports upset at the Call of Duty League Championship Weekend 2026 in Riyadh, the Riyadh Falcons handed a surprising defeat to the Los Angeles Thieves.

items.title: Hundreds Rally at Bethesda HQ to Protest Microsoft Layoffs Impacting id Software and Bethesda
items.summary: Hundreds of protesters gathered at Bethesda HQ to protest recent Microsoft layoffs that reportedly heavily impacted Bethesda and id Software, signaling growing industry tension over workforce reductions.

items.title: Wuthering Waves: Elysium Teaser Debuts via Kuro Games July 18 Livestream
items.summary: Kuro Games unveiled the first teaser for its animated project Wuthering Waves: Elysium and launched a new animation brand, Kuro Onroad, during a livestream on July 18.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero v3.1 Update Launching July 29 to Mark Second Anniversary
items.summary: Zenless Zone Zero will celebrate its second anniversary with version 3.1, launching on July 29, 2026, bringing new content to the game later this month.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple reclaimed the top spot as the world's most valuable company on Friday, surpassing Nvidia as investors reassess the AI market outlook.

items.title: Meta's Instagram and Facebook Suffer Sunday Outages
items.summary: Users reported widespread issues with Meta's Instagram and Facebook social networks on Sunday, affecting both the apps and websites.

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project aiming to deploy 1,000 satellites in total.

items.title: US Justice Department Allows Federal Employees to Download TikTok
items.summary: The U.S. Justice Department announced Friday that federal employees can now download the short-video app TikTok on government devices.

items.title: National Protests Erupt Against US Data Center Buildout
items.summary: Opponents of rapid data center expansion held coordinated protests on Saturday in at least 125 locations across the United States, marking the first national effort against AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide DoD with Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with access to data center capacity worth billions to run models, according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Judge Rejects Meta Employees' Bid to Block Layoffs Amid AI Discrimination Claims
items.summary: A U.S. judge rejected a bid by 26 Meta employees to block layoffs while they pursue claims that AI-powered tools targeted them for job cuts due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: Chip Stocks Face Steepest Weekly Decline in Over a Year
items.summary: A market rotation away from recent rally winners sent chip stocks toward their steepest weekly decline in more than a year, sparking concerns about the sustainability of the tech surge.

items.title: Valve Warns of Worsening Global RAM Shortage Impacting Steam Deck
items.summary: Valve warned that the global RAM shortage is worsening, impacting the affordability and availability of the Steam Deck and Steam Machine, with experts predicting the crunch could persist through 2027.

items.title: MLB Restricts Dugout iPad Use to Prevent AI Decision-Making
items.summary: Major League Baseball is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent artificial intelligence from influencing game decisions.

items.title: Brain-Chip Milestone: China Completes World's First Commercial Implant
items.summary: China completed the world's first commercial brain-chip implant, a coin-sized device marking a breakthrough in neurotechnology and a challenge to Elon Musk's Neuralink.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large Data Centers for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers that fuel technology expansion for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid.
